item.title: Project Sync 1.0.0
item.description:
Project Sync 1.0.0

New

  • First public release of Project Sync for scheduled one-way syncs between Mac folders, mounted network drives, and SSH-accessible machines.
  • Choose Backup mode to copy and update files without removing existing destination files, or Mirror mode when the destination should exactly match the source.
  • Preview changes before syncing, review plain-text logs and history, and cancel a running job when needed.
  • Schedule jobs manually, hourly, daily, weekly, or in response to changes in local and mounted-network folders.
  • Check for updates from the app menu, menu-bar menu, or Settings. Settings also lets you choose whether Project Sync checks for and downloads updates automatically.

Install & update

Existing users can use Check for Updates… in Project Sync.

New users: download Project-Sync-1.0.0.dmg, open it, and drag Project Sync to Applications.

item.title: Project Sync 1.1.0
item.description:
Project Sync 1.1.0

New

  • Add a Real-time schedule for Mac folders and mounted network drives. Project Sync syncs changes after the source has been quiet for two seconds.
  • Real-time jobs run once when watching begins to catch up on changes made while Project Sync or the source was unavailable. If files change during a sync, one follow-up sync is queued.
  • See real-time job status in the dashboard, including when a watcher is active, paused, or waiting for an unavailable source to return.

Fixed

  • Prevent creating a local or network sync when the source and destination folders contain one another, avoiding unsafe recursive copies.

Changed

  • Real-time watching is available for Mac folders and mounted network drives, including when syncing to an SSH destination. Remote SSH sources continue to use manual or timed schedules.

Install & update

Existing users can use Check for Updates… in Project Sync.

New users: download Project-Sync-1.1.0.dmg, open it, and drag Project Sync to Applications.

item.title: Project Sync 1.2.0
item.description:
Project Sync 1.2.0

New

  • Verify a job on demand, or have Project Sync automatically verify every successful sync with checksums. Verification never changes destination files.
  • Optionally keep versioned copies of files that a sync replaces or removes from a Mac or mounted-network destination. Browse archived versions and restore individual items; choose how many versions to retain.
  • Set a maximum number of simultaneous jobs. Additional syncs wait in a visible queue and start automatically as capacity becomes available.
  • Automatically retry temporary network, mounted-drive, and connection failures with configurable retry limits and backoff. You can also run eligible jobs whenever a source or destination volume mounts.
  • Pause a real-time watcher for an hour or until tomorrow, then resume it when ready.
  • Configure macOS notifications for sync starts, successes, failures, retry/waiting states, and backups that may be out of date.
  • Search run history, set a history retention limit, delete individual entries, clear a job’s history, or clear all history from Settings.
  • View transfer summaries in run history, including changed and transferred files, deleted files, data transferred, and speed.
  • Add notes to a sync job, duplicate an existing job for reuse, and add common exclusion presets for developer projects, macOS clutter, or caches.
  • Export a privacy-redacted diagnostic report to help troubleshoot Project Sync without including your file contents, paths, SSH usernames, notes, or exclusion names.

Changed

  • Project Sync now shows clearer queued, syncing, and verifying states, plus a brief on-screen confirmation when a sync or preview starts.
  • Real-time syncs wait for any active sync or verification to finish, then run one follow-up sync if source changes arrived in the meantime.

Install & update

Existing users can use Check for Updates… in Project Sync.

New users: download Project-Sync-1.2.0.dmg, open it, and drag Project Sync to Applications.

item.title: Project Sync 1.2.5
item.description:
Project Sync 1.2.5

New

  • Open View Live Sync… while a sync or preview is running to follow transfer output as it arrives, see current-file progress when available, reveal the log in Finder, or stop the transfer.
  • Verification now separates file-content differences, permission differences, other metadata differences, and destination-only items. Results include plain-language next steps and saved verification logs.
  • Choose whether permission differences must pass verification for each job. File contents are always checksum-verified; permissions can be treated as advisory when they are not meaningful for a destination.
  • Expand exclusion presets with ready-made options for JavaScript/TypeScript, Swift/Xcode, Python, Rust, Java/Kotlin, .NET, C/C++, Ruby, Dart/Flutter, and more.
  • Use optional exclusion presets for local environment secrets or source-control metadata when that fits the job’s purpose.

Changed

  • The Developer projects preset now excludes common dependency folders, caches, and build output across supported ecosystems while keeping source files, lockfiles, source-control history, and local environment files by default.
  • Jobs that use a mounted network drive now treat POSIX permission and directory-timestamp differences as advisory by default, avoiding repeated metadata updates on SMB and many NAS volumes. You can require permission matching in the job options.

Install & update

Existing users can use Check for Updates… in Project Sync.

New users: download Project-Sync-1.2.5.dmg, open it, and drag Project Sync to Applications.

item.title: Project Sync 1.2.6
item.description:
Project Sync 1.2.6

Changed

  • Closing the Project Sync window now keeps the app running in the menu bar instead of quitting, so scheduled and real-time sync jobs can continue in the background.
  • Use Open Project Sync from the menu bar whenever you want to bring the main window back. Choose Quit Project Sync from the same menu when you want to stop the app.

Install & update

Existing users can use Check for Updates… in Project Sync.

New users: download Project-Sync-1.2.6.dmg, open it, and drag Project Sync to Applications.
